patch.value: Defer eager per-command startup work: completion flag-strings, MCP tool schema build, telemetry flush spawn

Empty-project pm list CPU profile shows avoidable work: (1) completion.ts builds ~18 toCompletionFlagString constants at import (~3ms) only needed by pm completion; (2) sdk/cli-contracts.ts PM_TOOL_PARAMETERS_SCHEMA eagerly builds action-scoped MCP JSON schema at module load (~3ms) only needed by MCP/contracts; (3) telemetry scheduleTelemetryFlush spawns a detached node process on EVERY command (~6.5ms), deduped only by lock AFTER spawn. Make 1+2 lazy/memoized; debounce 3.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Addressed the highest-frequency sub-item: scheduleTelemetryFlush now spawns the detached flush child AT MOST ONCE per process (in-process guard). Previously command_start + command_finish each spawned a child (~6.5ms fork/exec each, deduped only by lock AFTER paying Node startup). One child drains the whole queue. DEFERRED with rationale: (a) PM_TOOL_PARAMETERS_SCHEMA lazy-build — it is public SDK surface (sdk/index.ts re-exports it, contract-tested in sdk-index.spec) and the eager build is only ~3ms (~1% of a ~300ms command); churning the public API for 1% is not worth it. (b) completion.ts flag-string constants are not on the bundled CLI hot path. The dominant per-command cost is ESM module-load/bundle, tracked separately under pm-ss1d (esbuild --splitting --packages=external). Keeping open for the bundling lever.

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: CORRECTION: reverted the in-process flush spawn-once guard. A flush child boots (~50-100ms) before even a normal command finishes (~300ms), so spawning only at command_start would systematically defer the command_finish event to the next invocation — a real-time telemetry timeliness regression not worth ~6.5ms. The correct optimization is a cheap cross-process 'flush already in flight' check before spawn (skip redundant spawns while preserving timeliness), tracked as future work. No telemetry runtime change ships in this PR; pm-3mal stays open for the bundling lever (pm-ss1d) + the in-flight cross-process flush guard.
